reviews.review_text: Have stayed here before. Lovely cabins with everything you need. Free mini golf and close to the water. Seamless check in and check out.
Great location as you're right next to the breakwall and saltmarsh. Cabin is small, but as to be expected. Couldn't fault reception staff.

Fantastic park. Park staff also very friendly and accommodating.

Perfect location. Access to the walking path along the bay and to the break wall and beach (around 10 mins). All flat. Bay is tidal and be swam or kayak. Great park down the path toward the main road.

Stayed in a cabin which was clean and new. All fit out was great. Small front deck to look at the water.

Kids enjoyed the pool. Water park suited to very young kids. Small games area, putt putt was a well designed course and also has communal kitchen, showers and laundry if camping.

Highly recommend.
The Caravan park is well maintained, the staff is friendly  and helpful could not ask for anything better.
Always loved coming here, our sites have been perfect, everything is easy to find in the park.

A mid week getaway for my birthday and the park did not disappoint. We took out van and booked a water facing site.
Park was clean, pool was great for the kids and enjoyed mini golf.
Plenty of walking tracks around.
We will be back.

Very pleasant stay. Lovely walks for our dog. We stayed in a roomy 2 bedroom dog friendly cabin. The only negative was no gate on the verandah. We would have loved to sit out there with her not having to tie her up.

I have stayed here many times and recent cabin upgrades are a nice improvement. However, the installation of smart TVs without any free-to-air capability via an aerial is disappointing. Guests are required to use their own mobile data, log in, and download apps just to watch TV. For cabins now priced at over $200 per night, access to standard free-to-air channels should surely be a basic inclusion. Guests without a phone with sufficient data or the technical capability to set up and access channels should book elsewhere.

Location was spot on. Accomodations were excellent, stayed in a cabin and it was extremely good quality. Plenty to do for the kids both inside and outside the park, especially the walking path to the council playground, first class playground. Don't forget the bikes or scooters well worth packing.
The ultimate family getaway, this holiday park was fantastic with so much for kids to do 👌🏽

great park with plenty to do but VERY isolated, with no shops on this side of town. the reception hours are 9-5 which is pretty un user friendly when it comes to needing change or supplies, and when I tried to purchase coins they wouldn't accept card and had no change machine for after hours... Zero bar OR food options so stock up before you arrive...
Staff were great though

G’day Parks Ballina is an awesome spot for a family getaway. The whole place has a really relaxed, welcoming vibe and it’s super family-friendly—plenty of space for the kids and a great atmosphere overall.

One of the highlights was the fishing. Being so close to the water made it easy to throw a line in, and it’s a great spot whether you’re experienced or just having a casual fish with the family.

The cabin we stayed in was really nice too—clean, comfortable, and well set up with everything we needed. It made the stay easy and enjoyable from start to finish.

All up, it’s a fantastic place to unwind, spend quality time with the family, and enjoy everything Ballina has to offer. Definitely a place we’d come back to!

10/10 .
Really wonderful.  Extended our stay and booked to stay again the following week or so.  Great sites, fantastic amenities ( ladies has a bath too, which is a big treat).
Lots of fun activities for all.  Best kids pool area Ive noticed.     Shops not far drive.   Really impressed
Everyone was friendly, the park was spotless and very relaxing.

This is a great park, situated on a lake/backwater of the Richmond river its close to a beach and an easy bike ride or 4K walk to town along the river. Bicycle track along river into town and also north along the coast, to some great beaches. You can go all the way to Lennox Heads not that we did.
Hello my kids very good behaviour

Couldn't recommend this place higher enough! Loved everything about it! Our cabin was clean, super comfy bed omg, thoughtful add ons throughout, well equipped kitchen etc etc perfect for what we needed. Wonderful view to the water.  The staff were kind & helpful. Will definitely be coming back next year.  Thanks so much for having us 😊
Great park right on the lagoon.
Great staff, apartment very clean, good facilities
Great place and location.
Good but
Nice location and quite place.

We could not be more grateful to the park manager who went over and above for us. Due to experiencing grief after losing our dear friend, I made a booking for the wrong date but Adrian graciously found a room for us that was beyond what we could have hoped for.

The cabin we stayed in was right by the water and had everything we needed. It was just so wonderful to experience the beauty of the surroundings at this difficult time. We had ample room which meant we could sleep comfortably; giving us the energy we needed for our travels en route from Brisbane back to the Central Coast.

We especially loved the bathroom products which were natural products and were grateful for the provision of supplies for tea and coffee etc.

We truly cannot thank you enough.

Great park
I wish I can stay for longer

We stayed here in January during peak summer. The park is well presented, and the cabins look nice and are equipped with everything we needed. It’s a great place for kids — the jumping pillow, games room, and the various-sized puddle pools were a big hit with our children.

However, there were some major downsides for us. The bathroom had a strong unpleasant smell, which was hard to ignore. We also have a 1-year-old who still naps during the day, and while there is air conditioning in the living area, there is none in the bedrooms. As a result, the rooms became unbearably hot, like an oven, making daytime naps nearly impossible.

Wi-Fi was essentially unusable — you’re much better off using your own mobile hotspot.

It’s disappointing to see that similar feedback has been mentioned in reviews going back years, with little sign of improvement. The park always seems to be fully booked, so perhaps there’s little incentive to modernise. We genuinely love the place and the facilities for kids, but until our children are older, we wouldn’t stay again — it’s just too difficult with a baby who needs to nap comfortably during the day.
Best park we've been too, we luv it there...
It's a places for families not singles. As its full of kids and dogs.
A nice quiet caravan park and the 2 bedroom cabin we had was comfortable.
Beautiful lake one side and a short walk and surf beach the other. Clean and tidy park. Lots for the kids to do. So relaxing

Beautiful stay! Big spacious cabin, very new looking! Big open verandah, bathroom is big, everything was spacious and new, loved this stay! Would definitely return. Close to water, lovely little walking paths, lots of nice play areas for the kids, overall great stay!
Great for kids jumping pillow, games room various size puddle pools, splashing warehouse, tunnels safe for little ones best if parents supervise.
Friendly and accommodating of our requirements. Can't beat the location.

Great caravan park on Shaws Bay, East Ballina. Well organised with permanent residents, cabins, and caravans in separate areas. Lovely gardens too.
Excellent facilities...we had an ensuite which was super clean, provided hand wash, hand lotion shampoo and conditioner. Very nice touch.
The closest bbq area again was spotlessly clean and provided washing up items.
Beautiful pool area and kids wading pool, free mini golf.
Highly recommended!!

Great caravan park.  I stayed here in my motorhome with my 2 young grandchildren and they loved all the activities, especially the putt putt and swimming pool/ kids water park.
The washing machines, apart from one take 5 x $1 coins.  Be sure to bring cash for some of the activities in the games room and washing machines.  Easy walking distance to the beach.

Wow! What a great place to stay!
We stayed 2 nights on a powered site in our motorhome. Sites small but manageable. Great walking tracks to the local coffee and takeaway place. Peacefull and amenities clean. Staff most helpful!
We will be back to stay longer 😃
We loved our stay on the waterfront caravan site with lovely views.  Nice walking tracks. This is our 2nd time we have stayed here and will definitely stay again.
Nice spot with a view. Great walking tracks along break wall.

Stayed here twice on this trip. First in a studio cabin with basic amenities, comfortable and clean. Went to sit at the outdoor  table and nearly fell - the seat support was broken.  Reported this via the QR  code and had someone there within half an hour.  Cannot complain about that service.  On our return visit a few days later there were no studio cabins left so we booked a delux cabin.  What a pleasant surprise that cabin was. Spacious, well maintained, clean and more than enough room for us, all at a very reasonable price.  The outdoor area was impressive with a dedicated undercover barbecue area for the exclusive use of the 4 surrounding cabins. This was a considerable improvement over the "resort" that we visited on the trip north.|I would recommend this park for anyone passing through or staying for a bit!
Fantastic park.

We booked Cabin 132 and Site 130 for 21–28 September and had it confirmed multiple times since January. On arrival, we were handed keys for Cabin 139. When I questioned this, Evelyn at reception was immediately rude and dismissive, stating “nothing could be done.” When we asked to speak with the manager, Richard, he was also unwilling to help, denying there were any notes on our booking (despite other staff later confirming there were). He also told us cabins were occupied when they clearly weren’t.

Evelyn’s behaviour throughout the week was unprofessional — including slamming keys on the desk — and we later discovered that many other guests had experienced similar issues with her. It’s disappointing that both Evelyn and Richard showed such poor customer service, especially when we had spent $1,700 for the week and have been loyal customers for years (over 17 stays in the past 3 years).

Sadly, the way we were treated has left us questioning whether to return. Discovery Parks Ballina needs to seriously address these ongoing staff issues
Nice local caravan park. Cabins are a bit small for 4, but nice outdoor seating. Very quiet.
Great place to spend few days. Kids got some good things to do. Mini golf, swimming pool, bouncing castle and all keeps them busy. Room is small but worth the money.
Was here for a huge event based in park.  Owners/employees went above and beyond to accomodate this. Great place to stay.
The park hosted a huge event with 100’s of women on motorbikes. They were amazing. The cabins were great and restocked daily

Wifi didn't work. No help provided. Main door couldn't be locked ; only insect screen could be locked. Reasonable cleanliness. Quite old. Very difficult to find our cabin as it the map provided was quite rudimentary and the street names were barely visible. It took us 30 minutes and more than 5 rounds around the whole bloody park to find our cabin! Ridiculous! Also very congested. Lots of people with caravans and motorhomes crammed into spaces as available. There are some permanent residents living within also which didn't give a vibe of living in a holiday home. I would NEVER COME HERE AGAIN! I reckon even a 2 star morel would be better option.
Other than sand fly bites, beautiful place and location
Am here for the 2WheelBabe Raid reunion and set the world record for most women on bikes

What a lovely place. Wish I had booked here.
Biker Babe Raid Reunion being held here Sept 2025

Great park with absolutely everything you could need for the kids, right on the water, very clean and in a great location!
Will be back for sure
Great lagoon caravan sites right near the river and walks.

Stayed in a superior cabin for 2 nights. Superior cabin it definitely wasn't. Old, poor amenities. I'd hate to see their standard cabin. |Blinds mouldy, ants in kitchen, Lukewarm water, very small bathroom. Wouldn't recommend this cabin.

Stayed one night in our motorhome on a powered water front site although there is a walking/cycling path between the site and the inlet.
Slab site but very small and felt surrounded by the other motorhome and caravans around us.
Facilities ok and it is good if you have bikes to cycle into Ballina or along the path to the headland.

The park is well presented and the amenities, although somewhat dated, are maintained to a high standard of cleanliness. We stayed on a powered site which was an adequate size for our larger caravan. We took advantage of the off-peak, winter offer, which was great value for money. The location of the park is excellent; just a stroll to a lovely beach and walking paths with beautiful coastal views. The attractive township of Ballina is just a few minutes drive away and has everything a traveller needs, including two outlets that sell fresh seafood. Enjoyable stay and would return.
Great park, clean amenities and very helpful and friendly staff 👍
Great place to stay, our cabin was clean & comfortable,  it had three bedrooms & two bathrooms,  also a washing machine.  Great value.
Dog friendly park which makes it easy when travelling. Only stayed one night, but was very happy with our ensuite site and the location.

We stayed at an ensuite site for a couple of days during the July school holidays. It was great.  Plenty of room around our site, very clean ensuite with everything you need including one of the best shower heads we've had in a caravan park!   Lots to do for families, mini golf,  while dated was great fun, a beautiful pool/water park area which I'm sure would be busy during the warmer months. We walked along the back path of the park every night down to the break wall and onto the beach. The other end of the footpath led us to a huge park and playground. Not owned by the caravan park but just a short walk. This caravan park is the perfect location with so much to enjoy, we will be back one day for sure.
Beautiful park on the river walking, bike ride ,fishing
A really nice quiet park. Riverside sites available. Reasonably priced with great pool, camp kitchen, mini golf and plenty of stuff for kids to do. Amenities older but spotlessly clean.

Absolutely horrible stay. We booked cabin 150 next to the mini golf. This cabin was not fit for rental!|Every aspect of it was disgraceful and falling apart. Bathroom was disgusting. Kitchenet was unusable and the car park was so small we couldn't park a standard 4x4 in there.  |The mini golf course was in horrible condition and also has what used to be a water feature that hasn't worked in a long time. The pond was full of moss and we could smell the stagnate water from our cabin.  |This place was so bad we didn't even stay for the amount of nights we paid for and we couldn't get a refund.

Absolutely horrible stay. We booked cabin 150 next to the mini golf. This cabin was not fit for rental!
Every aspect of it was disgraceful and falling apart. Bathroom was disgusting. Kitchenet was unusable and the car park was so small we couldn't park a standard 4x4 in there.
The mini golf course was in horrible condition and also has what used to be a water feature that hasn't worked in a long time. The pond was full of moss and we could smell the stagnate water from our cabin.
This place was so bad we didn't even stay for the amount of nights we paid for and we couldn't get a refund.

Stayed for 2 nights on a caravan site and found things very good. I actually returned for a further 5 nights on my way back from Queensland. I travel with my dog and can highly recommend this park as pet friendly with great walking tracks and it was a very peaceful experience.
Will stay again
Beautiful caravan park, staff were lovely and the powered sites were great. We will be back.
Budget cabin was dated. Noisy air con. Budget cabin, but paid less for better in other places.
Top spot

First stay at Ballina and we made a great choice to stay at the Ballina Discovery Park on a powered site with an ensuite. Site also had a concrete slab that was very useful given the rain we had over the four days of our stay. Site is short walk to the beach and partly backs onto the lake/lagoon and parkland.

Lovely park, nice location.
Staff are lovely and  helpful, except for the grumpy older woman at front office who was curt and abrupt on the phone and in person.
This park suits families with younger kids pre teen.
The arts and crafts were great and well planned/ thought out.
The pool is cold, just a heads up.
The reason for my negative review and the reason I will never go back is the camp kitchen closing overnight. It shuts at 9pm and does not open till 8am. Therefore overnight you do not have access to a fridge, kettle, microwave etc. As a camper you have to plan for what you will need overnight ie: water, food, hot drinks. So if camping be prepared to bring an Esky, ice, thermos etc. We were unaware of the closure and unprepared. We have been to caravan parks all over Australia and never not had access to a fridge, kettle, microwave overnight. I'm glad I didn't have a baby with me that needed bottles warmed in the microwave overnight.
So at 9pm, it's pouring rain and you get kicked out of the camp kitchen, there is no where for you to go. You are forced to retreat to your tent. It didn't make for an enjoyable evening.
Nor does waking up at 5.30am and having to wait till 8am for the kitchen to open so you can have a coffee.
If you want to leave early on your leaving day remember the kitchen isn't open till 8am, so no early breakfast before you leave.
K
Great place, wonderful staff
Great place to relax ,very nice park something for all ages ,and we love love the location 💓
Absoloutly stunning campground surrounded by water , Lake , river , Ocean . Ballina is a great town to visit peaceful and easy to get around .

We stayed 3 nights in a deluxe cabin with lake views, our cabin was very clean with everything needed for a perfect stay, a comfortable bed huge bonus. Brilliant sunsets at the mouth of the river made leaving hard, the check in and check out staff were very pleasant so to the cleaning and ground staff. Should feel proud of yourselves, I applaud your work ethics. Absolute gem of a place quiet and laid back, we will definitely be back to stay any time we are in Ballina. Well deserving of 5 stars
Len

We stayed here for a week. 4 days in a Superior 2 bedroom cabin and 3 nights in a standard cabin. The 2 bedroom cabin was the highlight. It was like living in a small apartment. The standard cabin would be ok for a 1 or 2 night stay, but for a longer stay, I'd be going for something bigger. The Park is ideally located in Ballina and walking distance to the North Wall, the Shawsy Hotel which was unfortunately closed for renovations and Lighthouse Beach and Shelley Beach. It' easy to see why there are a lot of permanent residents there.
Nice park with river frontage. Quiet location. Clean ensuite.
Had a great stay.
Staff were polite and friendly, rooms were lovely and clean, good selection of cooking utensils and well equip kitchenette. . .right next to the beach. . Highly recommend 🌟

A gem of a place. We stayed only one night in a 8 pax cabin and it was clean and very well equipped. Bed was comfortable and had complimentary coffee. Location is just beside the water and there is an excellent walking path where you can take in the beautiful sights. Located only about 6 minutes drive from a large supermarket.

As a family booking for the park facilities we were extremely disappointed to find the main reason we booked, the kids water park, was closed in the middle of summer with no notice provided to us in advance or at check-in. No discount offered to us. Saying there was a notice on the website is ridiculous for people booking 12 months in advance, I have no need to view the website every week leading up to our stay.
We were also told to use our own data and run a hotspot for the TV as Wi-fi didn't cover the park. This was just for free to air services that were not working.
We stay at the Ballina discovery park each year. Great staff, cabins and location.  Highly recommend it.

Caravan park well set put. A number of permanents but ample caravan and RV sites. Quiet, clean park and amenities. Dog friendly and close to beach, river and bay. Shaws Bay hotel currently being renovated but close enough to CBD for restaurants and other services.
Family caravan park

Is good location and plenty of spots and close to the beach and lake beach but the nightmare on elm Street pinball machine tilted me for no reason when I was going for the super high score and the mini golf pretty old and tired but it free so can't do the complain too much

I been in caravan park.
Excellent place for families with kids

My wife thinks she’s too cool for a caravan park but the Kids loved it. Plenty to do there. The kids water play area is pretty tired and in need of improvement but the kids didn’t complain. They particularly liked the pool and games room. |The other park guests were friendly. |Cabins were adequate.
Nice spot, spenty of places to walk along the river.  Amenities clean and tidy. Would stay again.
Great well maintained park. Would highly recommend

Our favourite park, right on the lake, great facilities, lovely walking areas, well maintained grounds, friendly, helpful, accommodating staff. We have our own van so can't comment on rooms. Reasonably priced. Five stars from us!! Highly recommended.
This is my second stay this year the accommodation is nice and clean and tidy all the mod cons close to town and the water.relaxing😎✌️
Great park, pleasant staff, pool, children’s water park, putt putt golf, huge camp kitchen. Always good to come back.

Couldn’t have asked for a better place to take my family . I was pleasantly surprised just how great all the facilities were. We had a cabin right by the water , everything about it was beautiful from watching to the sunset to sitting by the water in the late afternoon with our fishing rods . The kids pool and play rooms were fantastic and kept the kids busy , we didn’t get time to use the mini golf but it looked fantastic . This place is close to everything which is so handy. The rooms were clean and spacious. Overall a five out of five star stay. We are already looking to book again. Thank you for a lovely stay
Great caravan park, clean and very friendly staff

I just want to say a massive thank you to the park for hosting a large group of dads and kids. Anthony went above and beyond in terms of looking after us. A massive thank you to everyone for making it so special.

The park was in a great location. Accomodation was tidy. The owner of the park was walking around the grounds and was happy to stop for a chat. Very friendly. They have a really cool putt putt set up that you can play any time for free. Heaps of nice places to walk to
The standard cabin felt like living in a shoebox, flooring was marked and stained and there were cockroaches in the cabin.

Convenient location
Lovely  park
Helpful staff

I would give zero stars if I could
The way we were treated yesterday after staying there since 6/7/2024 was in one word DISGUSTING!
We got kicked out without any notice, reason or explanation!
After being in this caravan park  for so many weeks we were told to leave and not even allowed an extra 24 hours to pack up
Very unprofessional treated like we were done something wrong!
No notice written or verbal “ just leave today” !
Manager really irresponsible and there was no excuse whatsoever for his attitude
Just bluntly “leave today”
Yesterday evening I walked in and checked the site we were and it was empty! The manager will find an excuse like  “customer didn’t turn up” or something
Today I lodged a verbal complaint with their head office in Adelaide
There is no possible excuse whatsoever for treating us this way
The manager asked my credit card details 2 weeks ago and told me he will do a direct debit every fortnight so there would be no issues with payments
he denies this but a payment from my credit card came out 2 weeks ago so he had the details for sure
Reception lady with dark hair in a bun wearing glasses is also very rude and blunt from day one but we ignored that
There is construction works inside and the noise is quite loud 7 days a week
Shower cubicles aren’t cleaned properly and in one of them  is a shelf for the shampoo falling down  since day one!
Walls r not wiped properly and there is grime in the corners everywhere
Sites aren’t that big either
A shame that such a nice spot has a
DISGUSTING AND
APPALING CUSTOMER SERVICE!
I wouldn’t recommend this caravan park to anyone if u like a good customer service which isn’t too much to ask

We had cabin 159. We live in the residents section of Discovery Park and booked in as we were having renovations. We just spent a quiet time as I am disabled. Very comfortable time. Clean cabin, only dealt with Jackie who was able to extendour our stay for 2 extra night and groundsman Brian very courteous and helpful.Thanks guys

Stayed on site 130, location of park is great, nice shady spot but the cabins behind us were being renovated. Friday is a weekday so no problem but Saturday and Sunday? Grinders, drilling, banging, it was a construction site, other visitors and permanent site members could not believe they put us there!!! As we left the park I let the owner/manager know, his reply ‘thanks for letting us know’ seriously you had no idea that was being renovated? Customer service and care factor was 0 so my review reflects that. Sad because it’s a great spot!!!
East Ballina a great stop. Close to the ocean . Nice and quiet with lots of permanents. Not many showers for itinerants.

Deluxe 2 bedroom, 2 bathroom cabins are well appointed and contain everything you need (except an oven). Clean and staff were friendly. Enjoyed the games room, jumping pillow and free mini golf. The BBQ areas are also close to these cabins. An excellent choice of accommodation for families.
Nicely set out caravan park  in great  location.  Staff very helpful.
Loved the park. Clean amenities.
Bit far out of town to bike ride but beautiful spot great bike paths round the water
Great weekend in waterfront cabin with family. We were welcomed and looked after exceptional.
Looking forward  to a few days here on journey south  to Mildura
Great place specially for kids

Good staff at front desk, was a bit of a hiccup with checking in however it was sorted out efficiently and timely. Clean overall- clean cabin, floors, bathroom and linens. Definitely family oriented and friendly especially during school holidays.

No hot water. I was thrilled when my comment was attended to immediately.  Thank you very much for your quick response. We have stayed another night and the showers are wonderful.  A lovely park friendly staff and great for families.

We have a large family and booked two deluxe cabins we've never been in Ballina before but it was quite beautiful however after getting to our cabins we had some issues.
Cabin 1 which I was staying in:
- Smelt like rabbit or wet animal and was so potent that I could still smell it the morning after being in it all night
- The floors are disgusting, they need a good hard scrub with a hard bristle brush and some soap, my 12 month old was crawling around in a light coloured suit which now needs to be scrubbed and soaked from the filth she picked up and the amount of Wipes used to clean the colour from the topsides of her feet was feral
- Cockroaches. In the rooms, kitchen and lounge room, some even came crawling from under the pod machine when we went to use it!!

Cabin 2 which my family was in:
- Smelt of flea powder on the exterior of the cabin
- Again with the floors they were so gross and definitely 2 shades darker then they should have been
- Again with roaches.

For the price paid for a one night stay in those two cabins is crazy with how gross they were overall

We also had issue with the arcade, we attempted to play some games but everything needed coin's. My siblings had notes but no coins, tried reception to trade change and there was no answer and there was no change machines or anything to swap it out luckily I'd missed going to the bank and had coins on me still so they could try and enjoy themselves. We couldn't even play pool as the ques are in reception, it wasn't really late either around 5:30-6pm so the kids were disappointed at that.

The splash pool needs a repaint/repair. Some very sharp spots where it's been chipped away at overtime and had some stuck to my daughters hand as she was crawling through the shallow spots

Overall we're quite upset over our stay and couldn't find anyone to discuss this with and I'd love to be able to look back at this review and see improvement if we were to stay in the cabins again, Ballina is a beautiful space so we'd love to come back and try it again but for a first time so disappointed.

The park staff at reception were welcoming and helpful. The 3br waterfront cabin was a great location and we were really happy with our choice. The cabin is basic and could do with improvements. For example the remote was missing for the bedroom TV, privacy screen between cabins verandahs would be nice and more towels/blankets would be helpful.

The park staff at reception were welcoming and helpful. The 3br waterfront cabin was a great location and we were really happy with our choice. The cabin is basic and could do with improvements. For example the remote was missing for the bedroom TV, privacy screen between cabins verandahs would be nice and more towels/blankets would be helpful.
Stayed in a caravan park

This park is lovely! Fantastic for families with a big emphasis on entertainment such as water park/pools, games room, mini golf and a gymnasium. We stayed on a waterfront site and the water was so lovely and warm in the bay to wade through. Lovely walking track also. Sites are decent in size. Huge laundry also. As usual, the wifi is non existent despite getting a code to log in but this is pretty much the norm at any park. We take our own now 😆
Well set out. Family friendly great staff and very friendly

If I could rate zero I  would,  as Discovery member most disappointed in lady running the show. We had caravan troubles night before which I  fixed late into night and limped in to park at 9am to see if we could book in or at least have shower and clean up. Receptionist stated book in only after 11am, no exceptions and you can wait till 11am. No compassion and sad reflection on Discovery parks.

Discovery off Fenwick st, receptionist a dark hair approx.mid 30's. I did not have reservation as we were originally scheduled to go to Coffs Harbour prior to breakdown. My repair was only temporary to get into Ballina, with plan changed to now stay at Ballina and arrange to have van fixed properly. All I  needed was to set up, hot shower and some rest considering I had been under van most of night on gravel road-based. Maybe my dishevelled state and dirt tainted her reasoning.  Not to worry, moved on and people like that forgotten
Alex

We got the delux cabin, and for the price… I expected better. Unfortunately the room was dirty, garbage under the beds, stained sheets, leaking toilet and the one older lady at reception was very unpleasant to deal with, very rude and not welcoming! We did however like the location and mini golf was fun.
Nice to visit with family
Great park
Fantastic cabin great park
Great location, very quite

Great location plenty to do along and around lake bike around scooter too. Pool cold still this time of year. Games room plenty to do. Highly rated. Showers poor takes a while to adjust temperatures especially for kids. Washer dryer needs sign on wall displaying time and coins it takes. Mini golf needs a lot of attention.
Otherwise we enjoyed our stay here.
Great place to stay especially for children
Parking on opposite side of cabin entrance a problem in dark. Floor tiles very outdated pattern makes floor look dirty when not. Toilet is very low like childs.

Nice Caravan Park, the ensuites for each caravan, is a great thing, and a very well designed and functional park
Hopefully, we will be back.

Nice dog friendly park. All sites seem to be an easy walk to the beach. Very clean. Amenities were spotless every time I visited. Powered site essential in Qld summer. Huge amount of rain while we were there and there was no sign of any flooding.
What you would imagine from a caravan park. Friendly happy staff and can't possibly get a better location to the water

We were lucky enough to get a last minute powered site on the way to the Gold coast on Boxing day as we were notified our accommodation had been pushed back due to storm damage where we originally had a booking.
Wendy, Ritchie and team went absolutely above and beyond and accommodated us. Our original booking was cancelled in the end and the team at Ballina really came through for my family. I cannot thank them enough! We stayed on a powered site, one of the newly renovated cabins (and they even stored our van for us) and an ensuite powered site. The kids pool area and play area were fantastic! My girls had an absolute ball. I'm so greatful that we didn't have to cancel our family holiday!

Our 23 ft van (10 meters to the draw bar) and our F350 truck, was booked onto a median strip between 2 roads almost on the corner. The van went from one edge of the road to the other. It took about 45 minutes to get out due to the fact that we had been placed directly in front of the garbage bins, dump point and maintenance shed. The 5 kph was not obeyed by anyone and cars trucks trailers and van wizzed up and down these roads with children on bikes scooters and carts as well as pedestrians. Very dangerous. Should be using this space as free green space for children and families, not squeezing everyone in to turn over the almighty dollar. The pool was also disgusting and not cleaned, finally closed for Christmas and Boxing Days due to algae. In our 6 months of travel to Broome and back, this is by far the worst experience we have had.
Great park in the best location in Ballina, well priced and friendly staff

We stayed 4 days, 3 nights, and it was enjoyable. The most basic cabin had everything we needed, the location was 5 minutes from everything, and if you're a beach person there are beaches all around, including behind the cabins. Comfortable beds, too.

The caravan park itself and the waterfront cabin were great, but every interaction with the reception staff was horrible.
We had 2 families, one in cabin and another on a powered site, and every interaction that both families had with reception were terrible.
We rang the day before arrival to ask if we could be allowed in the cabin at 2pm and were just told that it will be ready when it's ready. We explained that we had an 8 month old baby and a 2 year old and were travelling for 7 hours to get there and we were hoping our cabin could be ready early, but they said 'everyone wants to get in early, not just you' and 'we have to stagger the check in's so it isn't too overwhelming for us' .. they didn't even check the cabin number to see if it was occupied the night before (which It wasn't)
When we wanted to hire the little bike things we enquired about booking one for all of us to use for a couple of hours and were told 'you won't want it for more than an hour' so we just didn't book.
The only positive interaction with reception was when we were leaving and handing the keys in.. go figure.

Even booking the cabin and site 6 months prior was an inconvenience to the staff and they made it difficult. My mum was paying for all accomodation but they wouldn't let us book and pay together and just made it difficult.

This is the second time staying at this place and both times the staff have been unpleasant. I won't stay again.

Also as others have said, the bug bites are terrible. It's been nearly 2 weeks and we all have many itchy bites all over us. Not the caravan parks issue of course, but an issue with where it is located.

Location is lovely, very quiet.
We booked a water front villa. Unfortunately it was not like the photos, we could see the water but not in front of our cabin, it was completely blocked by trees and shrubs. This was very disappointing considering the amount we paid for the cabin.
The majority of the cabin was clean but found dirty plates and utensils in the kitchen and broken crackers on the floor.  We had a big issues with insects resulting in lots of bites! Yes we used lots of repellent but after reading other reviews I see it’s been an ongoing issue.
The day we went to use the pool it was so dirty and cloudy, admittedly it was towards the end of the day but definitely not a pleasant experience.
The playground was great, the putt putt looked great, the toddlers play area wasn’t suitable for little ones as there was lots of broken toys. The games room was great.
Reception staff seemed very friendly and helpful
Favourite place to holiday. We try to get down to ballina every Easter when we can. Great location quiet and relaxing. Staff a lovely and always helpful. Best place for kids and big kids.
Absolutely lovely place. Pet friendly.  Great for kids. Service was fantastic. We will be going back. Hopefully with the Grandchildren.
Staff were amazing, couldn’t get over how big the cabin was and how clean, it convinced my children we had moved into a new house.
Great location and easy access. Definitely recommend this park. I rebooked for next year!

Overall neat tidy place.  Checking was friendly.  Tight access to site, even with a medium size van and not helped by narrow driveways.  Water pressure in showers was good though hot water was warm rather than hot. .
Studio cabin, sleeps 2 people, very clean & comfortable. Receptionist on checkin was very friendly. Would defiantly stay here again.
Good park quiet but squishy for larger vans.
Love Discovery Park, big place♡
Loved staying in this park. Friendly staff and great service. We stayed in a cabin and it was very pleasant and quiet. Close to the waters edge. A friendly park for a family to stay in.
Nice quiet park .no shops around but nice beaches lake and sea not far .
Great little park in town!
What a great place.
